title: Cautiously bullish then downward pressure: Q2 Cobalt Price Review
gist: Cobalt sentiment flipped from cautiously bullish to bearish over Q2 2026 as weak downstream battery demand overwhelmed Q1's supply-tightness narrative, though DRC export restrictions kept spot liquidity minimal and Benchmark's CIF Asia cobalt hydroxide index still closed the quarter virtually flat.

themes.theme: Cobalt Q2 2026 price and sentiment shift

insights.claim: Cobalt market sentiment shifted over Q2 2026 from cautiously bullish to a more dampened, bearish tone as weak downstream battery demand outweighed the supply-side support that had underpinned prices in Q1.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: The demand-side deterioration overtaking supply tightness suggests cobalt price risk is now skewed to the downside into Q3 unless downstream restocking resumes.
insights.quote: cobalt prices have been characterised by growing downward pressure, as the supply-side support that defined Q1 proved insufficient to counteract the widespread weakness in spot buying

insights.claim: The DRC's restriction of cobalt exports kept spot market liquidity minimal throughout Q2 2026, even as downstream demand weakened.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: DRC export policy remains the key supply-side swing factor for cobalt prices; any easing or tightening of restrictions should be watched as a near-term price catalyst.
insights.quote: spot liquidity stayed minimal as a result of the DRC’s restriction of exports

insights.claim: A lack of restocking interest from downstream buyers, combined with restricted feedstock availability, pressured cobalt sulphate pricing specifically as Q2 2026 progressed.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Cobalt sulphate (the precursor-chemical form used in cathode production) weakness is a more direct read-through to cathode/precursor margins than metal prices alone.
insights.quote: while feedstock availability remained restricted, the absence of restocking interest pressured cobalt sulphate pricing as the quarter progressed

insights.claim: Benchmark's CIF Asia cobalt hydroxide index closed Q2 2026 virtually flat compared to its opening price, despite the underlying shift in sentiment from bullish to bearish during the quarter.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: The flat headline index masks a deteriorating trend line intra-quarter; investors relying only on quarter-over-quarter index moves would miss the negative momentum building into Q3.
insights.quote: Benchmark’s CIF Asia cobalt hydroxide index closed the period virtually flat compared to its opening price.
